data.content:
### PostHog CLI Help Command

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/cli/README.md

Displays the main help information for the PostHog CLI, listing available commands and global options. This is the entry point for understanding the CLI's capabilities.

```bash
> posthog-cli --help
The command line interface for PostHog 🦔

Usage: posthog-cli [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  login      Interactively authenticate with PostHog, storing a personal API token locally. You can also use the environment variables `POSTHOG_CLI_TOKEN`, `POSTHOG_CLI_ENV_ID` and `POSTHOG_CLI_HOST`
  query      Run a SQL query against any data you have in posthog. This is mostly for fun, and subject to change
  sourcemap  Upload a directory of bundled chunks to PostHog
  help       Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --host <HOST>  The PostHog host to connect to [default: https://us.posthog.com]
  -h, --help         Print help
  -V, --version      Print version

```

--------------------------------

### Run Toolbox CLI (Python)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/infra-scripts/clitools/README.md

Executes the `toolbox.py` script using the Python interpreter. This command is used to start the PostHog toolbox CLI, optionally with flags to customize behavior like claim duration.

```bash
python toolbox.py [flags]
```

--------------------------------

### PostHog CLI Environment Variables for Authentication

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/cli/README.md

Defines the environment variables used for authenticating the PostHog CLI in CI/CD pipelines. These variables allow non-interactive authentication by specifying the host, API token, and environment ID.

```bash
# POSTHOG_CLI_HOST: The PostHog host to connect to [default: https://us.posthog.com]
# POSTHOG_CLI_TOKEN: [A posthog person API key.](https://posthog.com/docs/api#private-endpoint-authentication)
# POSTHOG_CLI_ENV_ID: The ID number of the project/environment to connect to. E.g. the "2" in `https://us.posthog.com/project/2`

```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies (macOS)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/common/hogql_parser/CONTRIBUTING.md

Installs necessary libraries for developing `hogql-parser` on macOS using Homebrew.

```bash
brew install boost antlr4-cpp-runtime
```

--------------------------------

### Run Toolbox CLI Directly (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/infra-scripts/clitools/README.md

Executes the `toolbox.py` script directly on Unix-based systems after making it executable. This is an alternative to running it via the `python` interpreter.

```bash
./toolbox.py [flags]
```

--------------------------------

### Install Playwright Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/playwright/README.md

Command to install Playwright, a necessary dependency for running the end-to-end tests. This should be run if Playwright is not already installed or needs to be updated.

```bash
pnpm --filter=@posthog/playwright exec playwright install

```

--------------------------------

### Install hogql_parser Locally (macOS)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/common/hogql_parser/CONTRIBUTING.md

Installs the `hogql_parser` package by building it from local source code on macOS.

```bash
pip install ./common/hogql_parser
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies (Ubuntu)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/common/hogql_parser/CONTRIBUTING.md

Provides a link to a GitHub Gist for installing Antlr runtime on Ubuntu, as the default package might be an older version causing compilation issues.

```bash
In that case run commands from [this step](https://github.com/PostHog/posthog/blob/4fba6a63e351131fdb27b85e7ba436446fdb3093/.github/actions/run-backend-tests/action.yml#L100).
```

--------------------------------

### Install MS SQL Drivers with Homebrew

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/posthog/warehouse/README.md

Installs the necessary Microsoft ODBC drivers for SQL Server on macOS using the Homebrew package manager. This is a prerequisite for connecting SQL data sources to PostHog. It requires accepting the End User License Agreement (EULA) before installation.

```bash
brew tap microsoft/mssql-release https://github.com/Microsoft/homebrew-mssql-release
brew update
HOMEBREW_ACCEPT_EULA=Y brew install msodbcsql18 mssql-tools18
```

--------------------------------

### Example AWS CLI Command for S3 Data Deletion

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/rust/capture/docs/llma-capture-overview.md

This snippet demonstrates how to use the AWS Command Line Interface (CLI) to recursively delete all objects within a specified S3 bucket prefix. This is useful for removing all data associated with a particular team.

```bash
aws s3 rm s3://posthog-llm-analytics/llma/123/ --recursive
```

--------------------------------

### Install pymssql from Git for Apple Silicon

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/posthog/warehouse/README.md

Installs the pymssql library directly from its master Git repository. This command is specifically recommended for Apple Silicon machines experiencing persistent issues with the pymssql installation, potentially resolving compatibility problems.

```bash
uv add git+https://github.com/pymssql/pymssql@master
```

--------------------------------

### Install Benchmark Tools (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/ee/benchmarks/README.md

Installs necessary tools for running benchmarks, including airspeed velocity and virtualenv. This is a prerequisite for local benchmark execution.

```bash
pip install asv virtualenv
```

--------------------------------

### Install ANTLR on macOS

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/posthog/hogql/grammar/README.md

Installs the ANTLR binary on macOS using Homebrew. Ensure you are using a compatible version, as specified in the project's CI configuration if a newer version causes issues.

```bash
brew install antlr
```

--------------------------------

### Make Script Executable (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/infra-scripts/clitools/README.md

This command makes the `toolbox.py` script executable on Unix-based systems. It uses `chmod` to change file permissions, granting execute rights to the owner.

```bash
chmod +x toolbox.py
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies and Run Storybook Locally (pnpm)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/frontend/src/stories/Hello.stories.mdx

This sequence of commands outlines the steps to set up and run Storybook locally within a GitHub Codespace. It includes installing project dependencies using 'pnpm install' followed by starting the Storybook server.

```bash
pnpm i
pnpm storybook
```

--------------------------------

### Install ANTLR on Ubuntu

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/posthog/hogql/grammar/README.md

Installs ANTLR version 4.13.2 on Ubuntu by downloading the JAR file and setting up necessary environment variables. This script configures Java, downloads the ANTLR JAR, and adds it to the CLASSPATH and PATH.

```bash
export ANTLR_VERSION=4.13.2

sudo apt-get install default-jre
mkdir antlr
cd antlr
curl -o antlr.jar https://www.antlr.org/download/antlr-$ANTLR_VERSION-complete.jar
export PWD=`pwd`
echo '#!/bin/bash' > antlr
echo "java -jar $PWD/antlr.jar $*" >> antlr
chmod +x antlr
export CLASSPATH=".:$PWD/antlr.jar:$CLASSPATH"
export PATH="$PWD:$PATH"
```

--------------------------------

### Install PostHog Python Library

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/wiki/integrations/Python-integration

Installs the Posthog-Python library using pip. This is the first step to integrate PostHog into a Python application.

```bash
pip install posthog
```

--------------------------------

### Build and Run PostHog from Source

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/wiki/Deployment

This sequence of commands installs and runs PostHog directly from its source code. It requires Python 3.7+, pip, and Yarn. The process involves cloning the repository, building frontend assets with Yarn, installing Python dependencies, and starting the application using Gunicorn.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/posthog/posthog.git
yarn build
pip install -r requirements.txt
gunicorn posthog.wsgi --config gunicorn.config.py --log-file -
```

--------------------------------

### Install easyjson for Go

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/livestream/README.md

Installs the easyjson tool, which is required for regenerating Go marshaller/unmarshaller methods when modifying fields commented with `//easyjson:json`.

```bash
go install github.com/mailru/easyjson/...@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Install npm dependencies for Amplitude Test Data Generator

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/rust/batch-import-worker/scripts/README.md

Installs the necessary Node.js dependencies for the Amplitude test data generator script. This is a prerequisite for running the generation and sending functionalities.

```bash
cd rust/batch-import-worker/scripts
npm install
```

--------------------------------

### One-Time Setup for PostHog Development Workflow (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/docs/FLOX_MULTI_INSTANCE_WORKFLOW.md

Installs necessary tools (direnv, gh, jq), configures the shell to use direnv for automatic environment loading, and sources the `phw` helper script for auto-cd functionality. Finally, it activates the Flox environment in the main PostHog repository.

```bash
# Install dependencies
brew install direnv gh jq

# Add direnv hook to your shell (~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc)
eval "$(direnv hook zsh)"  # or bash

# Add the phw function for auto-cd functionality
echo 'source ~/dev/posthog/posthog/bin/phw' >> ~/.zshrc  # or ~/.bashrc

# Reload your shell
source ~/.zshrc  # or ~/.bashrc

# Verify setup in main repo
cd ~/dev/posthog/posthog
flox activate
# You should see Flox environment activate and uv sync run
```

--------------------------------

### Quick Setup Script for Zsh

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/docs/FLOX_MULTI_INSTANCE_WORKFLOW.md

A one-time setup script for Zsh users. It installs necessary tools (direnv, gh, jq), configures direnv and phw sourcing, and provides confirmation of completion.

```bash
# For zsh users
brew install direnv gh jq && \
echo 'eval "$(direnv hook zsh)"' >> ~/.zshrc && \
echo 'source ~/dev/posthog/posthog/bin/phw' >> ~/.zshrc && \
source ~/.zshrc && \
echo "✅ Setup complete! You can now use 'phw' commands."
```

--------------------------------

### Install PostHog NodeJS Library

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/wiki/integrations/Node-integration

Instructions for adding the PostHog NodeJS library to your project using either npm or yarn package managers.

```shell
npm install posthog-ruby
yarn add posthog-ruby
```

--------------------------------

### Quick Setup Script for Bash

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/docs/FLOX_MULTI_INSTANCE_WORKFLOW.md

A one-time setup script for Bash users. It installs necessary tools (direnv, gh, jq), configures direnv and phw sourcing, and provides confirmation of completion.

```bash
# For bash users
brew install direnv gh jq && \
echo 'eval "$(direnv hook bash)"' >> ~/.bashrc && \
echo 'source ~/dev/posthog/posthog/bin/phw' >> ~/.bashrc && \
source ~/.bashrc && \
echo "✅ Setup complete! You can now use 'phw' commands."
```

--------------------------------

### Run Local Tests (macOS)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/common/hogql_parser/CONTRIBUTING.md

Executes the tests for the `hogql` module using pytest after installing the locally-built `hogql_parser` on macOS.

```bash
pytest posthog/hogql/
```

--------------------------------

### Install PostHog JS via npm or yarn

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/wiki/integrations/JS-integration

Integrate the posthog-js library into your project using npm or yarn. After installation, import and initialize the library within your JavaScript files.

```bash
yarn add posthog-js
```

```bash
npm install --save posthog-js
```

```js
import { posthog } from 'posthog-js';
posthog.init("[your-token]", {api_host: 'https://[your-instance]'});
```

--------------------------------

### Bash: Debugging Flox and Git Worktrees

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/docs/FLOX_MULTI_INSTANCE_WORKFLOW.md

Provides essential commands for debugging issues related to Flox environments and Git worktrees within the PostHog project. 'flox list' and 'flox search --installed' help inspect the current Flox environment, while 'git worktree list' confirms the status of Git worktrees.

```bash
# Check Flox environment status
flox list

# See what's installed in current environment
flox search --installed

# Check current worktrees
git worktree list
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshoot pymssql Installation

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/posthog/warehouse/README.md

Reinstall the pymssql library from source without using cached binary distributions. This is a troubleshooting step for resolving 'symbol not found in flat namespace '_bcp_batch'' errors when connecting to SQL databases. It ensures a clean installation from the latest available code.

```python
pip install --pre --no-binary :all: pymssql --no-cache
```

--------------------------------

### Run PostHog Log Capture Service from Source

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/rust/log-capture/README.md

This command builds and runs the PostHog log capture service binary from the source code using Cargo. Ensure you have Rust and Cargo installed.

```bash
cargo run --bin log_capture
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Use Cargo Watch for Live Testing

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/rust/feature-flags/README.md

Installs 'cargo-watch' via Homebrew for automatically re-running tests when code changes are detected. It then demonstrates how to use 'cargo watch' to continuously test the 'feature-flags' package.

```sh
brew install cargo-watch
```

```sh
cargo watch -x test --package feature-flags
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to Pod (Default Duration) (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/infra-scripts/clitools/README.md

Connects to a PostHog toolbox pod using the default claim duration of 12 hours. This is the simplest way to start a session with a toolbox pod.

```bash
python toolbox.py
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to Pod (Custom Duration) (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/posthog/posthog/blob/master/infra-scripts/clitools/README.md

Connects to a PostHog toolbox pod, specifying a custom claim duration in hours using the `--claim-duration` flag. This allows for shorter or longer sessions than the default.

```bash
python toolbox.py --claim-duration 4
```
